tasks.title: Fix ralph queue next-id to work with duplicate IDs
tasks.description: The "ralph queue next-id" command currently fails when the queue has duplicate task IDs because it calls load_and_validate_queues() which enforces ID uniqueness. This is problematic because users need this command precisely when they have duplicate IDs and want to find the next available ID to fix the collision. The command should be resilient and work even when the queue is in an invalid state with duplicates. Additionally, ensure documentation clearly states that task IDs must be unique across both queue.json AND done.json.
evidence: User report: "I had a duplicate task ID so I tried to run this command to see what the next ID was to switch the duplicate and it gave me an error"
next_id.rs line 44: calls load_and_validate_queues(resolved, true) which validates queue and will error on duplicates
docs/queue-and-tasks.md section on Duplicate Task ID Errors shows the intended workflow: use next-id to fix collisions
.ralph/README.md lines 15-23: Documents the duplicate ID error and the fix workflow using next-id
plan: Modify next_id.rs to handle duplicate ID errors gracefully:
- Instead of failing on validation errors, extract existing IDs from both queue.json and done.json
- Find the maximum ID number even when duplicates exist
- Return the next sequential ID based on max found
- Optionally warn about duplicates found while still returning the next ID
Update docs/queue-and-tasks.md Task ID Validation section:
- Explicitly state that task IDs must be unique across BOTH queue.json AND done.json
- Clarify that completed task IDs in done.json are included in uniqueness checks
- Ensure the "Fixing ID Collisions" section is clear and accurate
Update .ralph/README.md Duplicate Task ID Error section if needed for consistency
Add tests for next-id with duplicate IDs scenario:
- Test that next-id returns correct next ID even when duplicates exist
- Test that next-id considers done.json IDs even when queue has errors
Run make ci to verify all tests pass
tasks.request: The "ralph queue next-id" command is flawed. I had a duplicate task ID so I tried to run this command to see what the next ID was to switch the duplicate and it gave me an error. This command should work properly even if the queue is invalid with duplicate IDs so the user can find the proper next ID to update the errant ID. We should also ensure the docs are clear that duplicate IDs are NOT allowed and that completed task IDs in the done.json file are included in the checks for duplicate IDs. Task IDs must be unique across both queue.json and done.json.

tasks.title: Add conflict detection for external task changes during editing
tasks.description: TaskDetailView does not detect when a task has been modified externally (via CLI or another app instance) while the user is editing it. This can lead to data loss when the user saves, overwriting the external changes. The app should warn users about conflicts and offer merge options.
evidence: TaskDetailView.swift lines 31-42: Draft task created from initial task, no update tracking
File watching triggers reload but doesn't check if user is currently editing
No mechanism to compare task version at save time vs. edit start time
plan: Store original task checksum/version when editing begins
On file change notification, compare current task with draft
Show conflict alert if task changed externally with options: overwrite, discard changes, or merge
Implement merge view showing diff between local and external changes
Add 'modified externally' indicator in UI when conflict detected
Write tests for conflict detection scenarios
Verify by: open task in app, modify via CLI, attempt save in app - conflict should appear

tasks.title: Add error recovery UI for failed CLI operations
tasks.description: When CLI operations fail (network issues, corrupted queue, permission errors), the app shows a simple error message but offers no recovery options. Users cannot retry, view detailed error logs, or get guidance on fixing the issue. This leaves users stuck when errors occur.
evidence: WorkspaceView.swift lines 284-290: Simple error display with no recovery actions
TaskListView.swift lines 225-232: Error shown but no retry mechanism
All error handling shows static text without actionable recovery steps
plan: Create reusable ErrorRecoveryView with retry, diagnose, and dismiss actions
Add detailed error logging with 'Copy Error Details' button
Implement retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
Add 'Diagnose' button that runs ralph queue validate and shows results
Show contextual help based on error type (permission, parse, network)
Add 'Open in Terminal' button to debug CLI issues manually
Write tests for error recovery flows
Verify by: induce CLI error (bad permissions), verify recovery UI appears with retry option

tasks.title: Add offline mode detection and graceful degradation
tasks.description: The macOS app does not detect when the bundled ralph CLI is unavailable or when the workspace directory is inaccessible. The app shows loading spinners indefinitely or crashes without clear feedback. The app should detect offline/unavailable state and show appropriate UI.
evidence: RalphCLIClient.swift lines 265-278: Validates executable but no ongoing availability checks
Workspace.swift loadTasks() hangs indefinitely if CLI fails to respond
No timeout handling for CLI operations in the UI layer
plan: Implement CLI health check on app launch and workspace switch
Add timeout (30s) for all CLI operations with user-cancelable progress
Create OfflineBannerView to show when CLI is unavailable
Add 'Retry Connection' button with visual feedback
Cache last known task list for offline viewing (read-only mode)
Show appropriate messages for different failure modes (no CLI, no workspace, permission denied)
Write tests for offline behavior simulation
Verify by: remove/move ralph binary, launch app - should show offline state with retry

tasks.title: Add menu bar extra for quick task access and status
tasks.description: Implement a menu bar extra (status bar item) that provides quick access to Ralph without opening the full app. Show current task status, next task title, and quick actions (run next, add quick task, open full app). This enables lightweight interaction for users who want to monitor and control Ralph from anywhere.
evidence: No menu bar integration exists - app must be open to see status
Menu bar extras are standard for background/utility apps on macOS
Users may want quick status without full app window
Menu bar provides always-available access
plan: Create MenuBarExtra using App protocol or NSStatusBar
Add Ralph icon to menu bar with status indicator
Show current/next task title in menu
Add 'Run Next Task' menu item with keyboard shortcut
Add 'Quick Add Task' menu item that opens input dialog
Show task counts by status (Todo: 5, Doing: 1, Done: 12)
Add 'Open Full App' menu item
Implement preferences toggle to enable/disable menu bar extra
Show notifications for task completion via menu bar
Add recent tasks submenu for quick access
Verify by clicking menu bar icon and testing all menu items

tasks.title: Implement global keyboard shortcuts and command palette
tasks.description: Add comprehensive keyboard shortcuts for power users including Cmd+N (new task), Cmd+R (run next), Cmd+K (command palette), Cmd+1/2/3 (switch views). Implement a command palette (Cmd+Shift+P or Cmd+K) that provides quick access to all app actions with fuzzy search. This enables efficient keyboard-driven workflows.
evidence: No keyboard shortcuts exist beyond system defaults
Power users expect keyboard-driven workflows
Command palettes are standard in modern dev tools (VS Code, Raycast)
Shortcuts significantly improve productivity for frequent actions
plan: Define keyboard shortcut scheme (Cmd+N new, Cmd+R run, Cmd+F find, Cmd+K palette)
Implement keyboard shortcut handlers in window
Create CommandPaletteView with fuzzy search
Register all app actions in palette with descriptions
Add shortcut indicators in menu items and tooltips
Implement view switching shortcuts (Cmd+1 Queue, Cmd+2 Run, Cmd+3 Analytics)
Add escape key handling to dismiss modals/palette
Implement space key to preview selected task
Add shortcuts for status changes (Cmd+Shift+S for status menu)
Document all shortcuts in Help menu
Verify by testing each shortcut and confirming action fires
